title: B1 Listening - Part 1 - Test 9
instructions: For each question, listen to the recording and choose the correct answer: A, B or C.

items.context: You hear two friends talking about a tennis lesson.
items.audio_script: Boy: Are we still meeting on the outdoor courts for the tennis lesson? I wrote it in my diary, but I left my phone at home this morning. || Girl: That was the original plan, but the weather report says there may be heavy rain. [1]We need to meet on the indoor court instead[/1]. || Boy: I am glad I asked. I would have gone to the wrong place and wondered where everyone was. || Girl: That is all right. I will arrive ten minutes early, so look for me near the entrance. Keep your phone on in case anything changes again.
items.question: Where will the tennis lesson be?
items.explanation: The lesson will be on the indoor court.

items.context: You hear two students preparing for a forest walk.
items.audio_script: Girl: Have you packed for the forest walk yet? I put a camera in my bag because I thought that was what we needed. || Boy: That was on the first list, but there are lots of mosquitoes near the river this week. [2]Everyone needs to bring insect spray now[/2]. || Girl: I would have taken the wrong thing otherwise. I was about to leave my bag by the door and forget about it. || Boy: Do not worry; we are not leaving yet. Put it in now, and check the final message once more before we leave tomorrow morning.
items.question: What should the student take?
items.explanation: The student should take insect spray.

items.context: You hear two friends planning Saturday afternoon.
items.audio_script: Man: Are we still visiting a museum on Saturday afternoon? I told my sister I would be home later, so I need to know. || Woman: That was the idea, but the museum tickets have sold out for the whole weekend. [3]We are going to go bowling instead[/3]. || Man: Fine by me. I nearly paid for the other activity online this morning. || Woman: Luckily you asked first. I will book our places this evening and send you the time. We can meet at the bus stop after lunch.
items.question: What will they do on Saturday?

items.context: You hear two passengers discussing a train.
items.audio_script: Woman: I need to write down the time for the train to York. Is it still at ten past twelve, like you said yesterday? || Man: No, there is a short delay because of signalling problems. [4]It starts at twenty-five past twelve now[/4], although we should arrive ten minutes earlier. || Woman: I am glad I checked. I would have been waiting there at the wrong time. || Man: I will send you a reminder later. Bring everything you need, because there will not be time to go home again before it begins.
items.question: When does the train leave?
items.explanation: The train leaves at twenty-five past twelve.

items.context: You hear two people talking about a watch.
items.audio_script: Girl: How are you getting on with your watch? I heard it was not working properly. || Boy: I first thought the strap had broken, and my brother wondered if the glass was scratched. But he checked it carefully last night. [5]It needed a new battery[/5]. || Girl: Can it be fixed before the match tomorrow? || Boy: It should be possible. We know what is wrong now, and the repair shop said it should be ready tomorrow afternoon. That is a relief because I need it this weekend.
items.question: What was wrong with the watch?
items.explanation: The watch needed a new battery.

items.context: You hear two friends planning to go to the city centre.
items.audio_script: Man: Are you still travelling to the city centre by bus? I could meet you when you arrive. || Woman: I planned to, but traffic will be very slow after the match. I considered going by car, but parking costs too much there. [6]I am going by underground instead[/6]. || Man: That sounds sensible. What time will you get there? || Woman: Just before eleven, if everything is on time. I have already checked the timetable online, so it should be easy. I will message you when I leave, so you do not have to wait outside.
items.question: How will the speaker travel to the city centre?

items.context: You hear two people preparing for a birthday party.
items.audio_script: Boy: Did you buy some balloons for the party? I saw one in the shop window yesterday and thought it looked nice. || Girl: No. I looked at some balloons, and I nearly chose some candles, but my aunt has already decorated the room and we still need dessert. [7]I bought a birthday cake in the end[/7]. || Boy: That sounds like a good choice. It sounds more useful, and I think it will be appreciated. || Girl: I hope so. I kept the receipt, just in case it needs changing after we give it.
items.question: What did the speaker buy?
items.explanation: The speaker bought a birthday cake.
